title: Frontier results, on device - RL Nabors, Arize
summary: RL Nabors from Arize argues that most applications can use small local models instead of frontier models, reducing latency, energy, and cost. He presents a framework using capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge to measure when local models are sufficient and shares production examples including agentic systems, moderation classifiers, and summarization features.
writeup: RL Nabors of Arize makes the case that many production applications can run on small local models rather than frontier models, cutting latency, energy use, and cost. The talk outlines a measurement framework built on capability evals, golden datasets, and LLM-as-judge to determine when a local model is good enough for a given task. Concrete examples span agentic systems, moderation classifiers, and summarization features, grounding the argument in shipped use cases rather than benchmarks alone.
